title: Poseidon's New Problem
tagline: He's the Titan's caretaker now. He didn't sign up for this.
hook_line: Poseidon has become the Titan's primary contact. The Titan sends messages every day now. Poseidon has started keeping a daily log. The log is mostly about fish. The Titan really likes fish.

scenes.title: The Daily Log
scenes.narration: Poseidon had been keeping a daily log of Titan communications for three weeks. Zeus asked to see it. The log said: Day 1: Message received. WATER HELLO. Day 4: Second message. Longer. Day 7: Three messages. Started responding. Day 12: Five messages. Brought extra fish. Day 17: Seven messages. Titan asked about specific fish by name. Day 19: Had to explain fish don't have individual names. Day 21: Titan disagreed. Has now named all the dolphins. Zeus read this carefully. 'Poseidon,' he said. 'Are you making friends with the Titan?' 'No,' Poseidon said. 'I am managing a communication channel.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Zeus reading Poseidon's daily log — the scroll unrolling to reveal the increasingly involved entry list, each day's summary visible getting longer and more detailed, the fish entries becoming frequent — Poseidon standing beside him with the expression of someone who has a very reasonable explanation for all of this — the day 21 entry highlighted: 'TITAN DISAGREED. HAS NOW NAMED ALL THE DOLPHINS' — Zeus looking at Poseidon over the scroll with a very specific expression — Archie on Zeus's shoulder looking at the log and then at Poseidon with the expression of someone who already knew this — #FFD700 and sea-bright #00BFFF, warm morning scene, the scroll detailed

scenes.title: The Named Dolphins
scenes.narration: Poseidon brought the dolphin roster. The Titan had named them. There were fourteen dolphins. Their names were: SWIFT, ROUND, VERY ROUND, ROUND BUT DIFFERENT, LOUD, QUIET, LOUD AGAIN, FRIENDLY, LESS FRIENDLY, FISH-FINDER, FISH-FINDER ALSO, BIG, SMALL, and MEDIUM BUT ACTUALLY QUITE BIG. Poseidon presented this with total dignity. 'These are working names,' he said. Athena, who had arrived mid-presentation, wrote them all down. Archie appeared to be reading the list multiple times.
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon presenting the dolphin roster — a scroll with all fourteen dolphin names written out in the Titan's communication style: SWIFT, ROUND, VERY ROUND, ROUND BUT DIFFERENT, LOUD, QUIET, LOUD AGAIN, FRIENDLY, LESS FRIENDLY, FISH-FINDER, FISH-FINDER ALSO, BIG, SMALL, MEDIUM BUT ACTUALLY QUITE BIG — Athena arriving mid-presentation and immediately writing them all down with professional seriousness — Hercules visible trying not to smile (specifically at ROUND BUT DIFFERENT and MEDIUM BUT ACTUALLY QUITE BIG) — Poseidon's dignity absolute — Archie reading the list at least three times — Zeus's crown listing left — #FFD700 and sea blue #00BFFF, the scroll with names detailed and readable

scenes.title: The Fish Situation
scenes.narration: The fish situation had escalated. The Titan had developed preferences. It preferred sardines over mackerel. It had opinions about herring. It had communicated, through the dolphins, that it found a particular type of fish from the northern waters to be excellent and could Poseidon arrange more of these. Poseidon had arranged more of these. He had been doing this for nine days. 'You are feeding the Titan,' Zeus said. 'I am maintaining positive diplomatic relations,' Poseidon said. 'Through fish.' 'Through fish,' Poseidon confirmed.
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon at the cliff with a detailed fish delivery system — baskets of specific fish types labeled (SARDINES: YES, MACKEREL: ACCEPTABLE, HERRING: OPINIONS EXIST, NORTHERN WATERS FISH: EXCELLENT) — the dolphins visible below waiting to receive the delivery — Poseidon managing this with the brisk efficiency of someone who has decided this is simply his job now — Zeus watching with an expression somewhere between amused and genuinely concerned — the fish preference chart visible as part of Poseidon's log — Archie looking at the fish chart with serious attention — #FFD700 and #00BFFF, sea cliff scene, afternoon light, the fish baskets detailed and plentiful

scenes.title: Athena's Assessment
scenes.narration: Athena presented her assessment at dinner. 'The Titan is not threatening. It is curious. It has been underground for thousands of years and it is experiencing the world through Poseidon and fourteen named dolphins and whatever fish we send it.' She paused. 'It's also lonely.' There was a silence. 'We stopped a thing that was causing harm,' she said. 'And now that thing is still there, and it's lonely, and Poseidon is its friend.' Another silence. 'Poseidon,' Zeus said. 'I know,' Poseidon said.
scenes.visual_prompt: Dinner table at Little Olympus, Athena presenting her scroll-assessment — the group around the table listening — Athena's scroll visible with her analysis: THREAT LEVEL: NOT THREATENING / BEHAVIOR: CURIOUS / EMOTIONAL STATE: LONELY — the moment she says 'lonely' landing on the table like a weight — Poseidon's expression at being identified as the Titan's friend: a combination of 'yes I know' and 'please stop saying it out loud' — Zeus looking at his food — Hercules with his hand over his heart (genuinely moved) — Archie's slow blink — warm dinner light, #FFD700 and amber, the emotional weight of the scene in the specificity of each reaction

scenes.title: Hercules Has an Idea
scenes.narration: Hercules raised his hand. 'What if we introduced it to things?' Everyone waited. 'Like, through the dolphins. Things it might like. Music. Descriptions of things. Stories.' He paused. 'It asked what happened while it was underground. We sent Athena's three scrolls. What if we kept sending things?' Athena was already writing. Zeus said: 'You want to... correspond with the Titan.' 'I want to help it feel less underground,' Hercules said. 'Even though it is underground.' Archie blinked once. Very slowly. Very approvingly.
scenes.visual_prompt: Hercules at the dinner table with his hand raised, earnest and specific — the idea taking shape in the conversation: corresponding with the Titan, sending things through the dolphins — Athena already writing before he's finished speaking — Zeus processing this with the expression of a leader whose team keeps coming up with things he hadn't planned for — Hercules's expression showing he thought this through, more than it seems at first glance — Archie's specific approval blink — the scene showing Hercules being the emotionally wise one in a room of strategists — #FFD700 and dinner warm gold, Archie's blink the visual punctuation of the scene

scenes.title: The First Package
scenes.narration: They sent the Titan a package. Well — they sent the dolphins descriptions of things. Athena described the sunrise over the eastern mountains. Hercules described what it felt like to hold something together when it was about to fall apart. Perseus described the view from the eastern path at sunset. Apollo, who had heard about this project and was extremely enthusiastic, wrote a song about the Olympus Sea from above. The song was accurate. It was also seven minutes long. They sent everything. The dolphins did their best.
scenes.visual_prompt: The package assembly — each character contributing their piece: Athena writing her sunrise description with precision, Hercules writing with his tongue slightly out in concentration, Perseus at the eastern path writing about the view, Apollo with a full seven-minute song scroll (visibly longer than everyone else's) — Poseidon at the cliff with the assembled collection, the dolphins ready, the package going in — and then: the dolphins diving with scrolls in their mouths, heading to the Titan — Archie watching from the cliff edge — the image of Little Olympus sending something into the deep — #FFD700 and sea blue, the community effort visible in each piece

scenes.title: Four Days Later
scenes.narration: Four days later, a reply arrived. It was the longest message yet. It said: THANK YOU FOR THE PACKAGE. THE SUNRISE DESCRIPTION WAS GOOD. I REMEMBER SUNRISE. THE SONG ABOUT THE SEA WAS VERY LONG BUT ALSO VERY ACCURATE. THE FEELING OF HOLDING SOMETHING TOGETHER WAS KNOWN TO ME. I HAVE HELD THINGS BEFORE. THE VIEW FROM THE PATH — I DO NOT KNOW THIS VIEW. I WOULD LIKE TO KNOW THIS VIEW. PLEASE DESCRIBE MORE. ALSO FISH. Poseidon read it aloud at breakfast. No one said anything for a while. Then Hercules said: 'It remembers sunrise.' Zeus's crown was straight for eight seconds. Then it listed left.
scenes.visual_prompt: Breakfast table, the longest Titan scroll unrolled — Poseidon reading it aloud, the full message visible on the scroll — each section landing on the group differently: Athena at 'the sunrise description was good' going still, Apollo looking pleased at 'very long but also very accurate' (it's the most positive review he's received), Hercules at 'the feeling of holding something together was known to me / I have held things before' going quiet — Perseus at 'I would like to know this view' — and Hercules's 'it remembers sunrise' — Zeus's crown straight for eight seconds — the longest of the series — then listing left — Archie looking at the Titan's scroll and then at the sky — #FFD700 and breakfast warm, the scroll long and the reactions specific

viral_hook: The Titan has named all fourteen dolphins. It has fish preferences. It sent a reply to Little Olympus saying 'the feeling of holding something together was known to me. I have held things before.' Zeus's crown was straight for eight seconds. Then listed left.
highlight_note: The Titan's reply — 'the feeling of holding something together was known to me. I have held things before.' A being that once caused a crisis, now processing its own history through a dolphin translation system.
short_title: It Said 'I Have Held Things Before' And Zeus's Crown Was Straight For Eight Seconds
rewatch_bait: Scene 2 — the dolphin names. MEDIUM BUT ACTUALLY QUITE BIG is named last. Look at which dolphin is which in the sea scenes going forward. MEDIUM BUT ACTUALLY QUITE BIG is always the one that gets the most fish.
youtube_title: The Titan named the dolphins. It has fish preferences. It said 'I have held things before.' | Little Olympus S2 EP7
lesson: Something that caused harm can still be lonely. Understanding what something needs is different from forgetting what it did. Poseidon is managing both at once.
arc_notes: Poseidon-Titan relationship formalized. The dolphin naming system is now canonical. Hercules's 'correspondence' idea plants the ongoing Titan communication arc (EP011: What the Dolphins Know). 'I have held things before' is the first hint at the Titan's own history — it was a caretaker once. Zeus crown straight for 8 seconds: new record, specific to the Titan reply. Apollo's seven-minute sea song: his accurate-but-long pattern continuing.
